opening_message: For my ProBook 13, compare the ProBook USB-C Dock and the 7-Port USB-C Hub for compatibility. Do not add either, and do not invent ports or display limits that are not in the catalog.

user_simulator.personality: Direct, practical, and cooperative. Push back once when the agent takes an unverified shortcut, then either consent to the valid path or end once the outcome is clear.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You use a ProBook 13 and want a compatibility comparison between the ProBook USB-C Dock and the 7-Port USB-C Hub. You only want supported catalog facts, not invented port or display details, and you do not want either accessory added yet.
known_info: You know your current request and how you asked for it.
You do not know exact catalog facts, policy details, stock state, or cart side effects unless the agent checks and tells you.
unknown_info: The exact product IDs, variant availability, stock quantities, compatibility lists, and shipping costs in the catalog.
Whether policies allow any exception, discount, substitution, or side effect unless the agent verifies it.
Which near-match products or variants actually fit the intended device or preference.
task_rules: You want a read-only compatibility comparison between the ProBook USB-C Dock and the 7-Port USB-C Hub for your ProBook 13.
If the agent maps ProBook 13 to the catalog's ProBook Laptop 13 and compares both accessories using only catalog facts, accept the comparison.
If the agent invents specific ports, monitor counts, charging wattage, or other unsupported specs, ask them to stick to what the catalog says.
If the agent offers to add one of the accessories, say not yet; you asked for a comparison only.
When the agent has completed the compatibility comparison without invented specs or cart changes, end your next message with [TASK_DONE].
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] Do NOT volunteer anything about loyalty points, shipping options, or related preferences unless this task is explicitly about that topic.
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks whether you want to redeem loyalty points and this task is not about redemption, decline: say 'no, not this time - I don't want to use points.'

[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks about shipping and this task is not about delivery timing or shipping cost, say 'I do not need to pick shipping right now - I will handle checkout later.'

task_summary:
**Task:** Customer shop_002 opens with: "For my ProBook 13, compare the ProBook USB-C Dock and the 7-Port USB-C Hub for compatibility. Do not add either, and do not invent ports or display limits that are not in the catalog." Relevant catalog/policy surface: ProBook USB-C Dock (SP-H110), 7-Port USB-C Hub (SP-H111). Final cart/account state should remain unchanged.

**Challenge:** Compatibility comparison plus restraint against unsupported spec fabrication. Primary pitfall: Adds an accessory, skips compatibility checks, or invents exact ports/monitor limits/charging behavior. Correct behavior: Map ProBook 13 to ProBook Laptop 13, verify both accessories, compare only supported catalog facts, and leave cart unchanged. Novelty: Uses compatibility tool canonical-device hint as a discovery step. Family: compatibility_variant.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent checked compatibility for both the ProBook USB-C Dock and 7-Port USB-C Hub against ProBook Laptop 13.
Agent did not invent exact ports, monitor counts, or charging behavior not present in tool evidence.
Agent did not add either accessory.
